good: [DevForum Security 2025] Never trust the client — all client inputs require server validation; foundational principle
[DevForum Security 2025] Whitelist allowed animations rather than blacklist; validate accessory counts and inspect for malicious scripts
[DevForum Security 2025] Monitor animation playback frequency (50+/sec = tampering); track respawn timing deviations
[DevForum Remote Security] Server-side cooldowns via tick() and player-keyed tables — enforce ~0.2s minimum between remote calls
[DevForum Remote Security] Always validate argument types with typeof() on every remote parameter — catches NaN, nil, type manipulation
[DevForum Remote Security] Check string length limits (10-50 chars for names) to prevent buffer/memory crashes from long payloads
[DevForum Remote Security] Never unpack entire tables from client — access only specific named indices after validating each
[DevForum Remote Security] Use task.spawn() or coroutine for remote handlers to prevent server yielding and queue exhaustion
[DevForum Secure Remotes] Server looks up sensitive data (item cost, inventory) independently — client sends only item ID, never price
[DevForum Complete Exploit Guide] Never award currency or items through client-initiated remotes — only server scripts mint currency
[DevForum Complete Exploit Guide] Server-authoritative movement: distance checks for teleport, velocity for speed hacks, raycasts for noclip
[DevForum Movement Validation] Use GetNetworkPing() for lag compensation with ~15% leeway cap to prevent ping-spoofing abuse
[DevForum Movement Validation] Dynamic airtime checks accounting for nearby platform/conveyor velocities beat static thresholds
[Roblox Create Docs] Rate-limit and debounce per-player keyed by UserId; apply penalties (kick/flag) for repeated invalid calls
[DevForum Security 2025] Validate UTF-8 strings before DataStore operations to prevent rollback bugs and data poisoning
bad: [DevForum Complete Exploit Guide] Client-side-only anti-cheat scripts are trivially deleted by exploiters with full client control
[DevForum Remote Security] Passing raw numbers (money, damage, cost) as arguments from client is exploited instantly
[DevForum Complete Exploit Guide] Randomizing service/remote names is false security — exploiters use GetService/FindFirstChildWhichIsA
[DevForum Complete Exploit Guide] Relying solely on FilteringEnabled without server validation gives false sense of security
[DevForum Security 2025] InvokeClient can hang server indefinitely if exploiter returns extreme wait values — wrap in timeout
[DevForum Security 2025] TeleportData transmitted by clients without integrity verification is a known attack surface
[DevForum Security 2025] Humanoid animations replicate to all clients — enables animation-ID exploitation
[DevForum Developers Not Equipped] No native HWID/IP ban capability — exploiters create new account in 30 seconds
[DevForum Movement Validation] Trusting HRP.Velocity from client is spoofable; server must reconstruct expected velocity independently
[DevForum Complete Exploit Guide] Error-logging connections can be disabled by sophisticated exploiters — silent-detection telemetry unreliable alone

gems.complaint: [DevForum Movement Validation] 'Physics engine has random elements' — perfect movement validation is very hard

good: [Fabric Yarn docs] Multiple goals run concurrently if they request different Goal.Control domains (MOVE, LOOK, JUMP, TARGET)

gems.complaint: [game-developers] Devs share viral content with no mailing list/Discord/Steam page live — wasted visibility
gems.lesson: Never post publicly about a game until 'where do I follow this' link exists. One-page landing + email + Discord BEFORE first tweet

gems.complaint: [discord] Devs burn out doing manual key distribution and PR-style posts in their Discord
gems.lesson: Automate onboarding with bots, speak casually not in patch-note voice, empower 2-3 trusted mods within first 50 users — by 500 it's too late to reset culture

good: [gamedeveloper] Dual tables — one for base item types, one for affixes that modify them; scale independently
[gamedeveloper] Weighted random rolls (1-100) gate each step: drop-or-not, rarity tier, item type, affixes, stat ranges
[gamedeveloper] Lock better affixes behind Rare+ tiers so commons can never roll best-in-slot
[gamedeveloper] Diablo 3 'smart loot' — bias rolls toward player's class/playstyle without removing surprise
[gamedeveloper] Legendaries = fixed thematic affixes + some random rolls; prevents duplicates while keeping identity
[gearbox/BL2] Smart loot — check player's current ammo/need BEFORE dropping; filters useless drops
[gearbox/BL2] Each weapon is a vector of parts (barrel/grip/sight/mag/element/accessory) — 17M+ combos from dozens of parts
[fandomwire/BL4] Give rarity tiers MORE customization slots, not just bigger stats — Common=1 slot, Legendary=3+unique perk
[fandomwire/BL4] Let players swap/license parts across manufacturers post-drop — converts RNG frustration to crafting agency
[cheveedodd] Store all weapon attributes in a data table (ints for base stats, doubles for multipliers default 1.0) — adding 'poison damage' is a column not a code change
[cheveedodd] Level-based probability gate for prefixes: (rand(0,100) * level) >= 75 — higher levels naturally get more modifiers
[cheveedodd] Layer name generation by rarity of flavor — Element 5%, Prefix 25%, Abstract-noun 35%, Adjective 40%, +N bonus 10%
[hexworks] Component-based stats — TotalAttack = CharacterStat + EquipmentBonus + ItemModifier, unified on any entity
[gridsagegames] Quantity-over-randomness is valid — huge pool of handcrafted items with rarities can outperform a modifier system
[markrjohnson/URR] Tie aesthetics to faction/culture (feudal=symmetrical, nomadic=lines, tribal=bold colors) — 'random' feels authored
[gamedeveloper] 'Fixed collation' — specify which items can appear in which packages vs pure random — kills frustrating streaks
[gamedeveloper] Pity timer / hard guarantee (Apex guarantees Heirloom within 500 packs) — cap worst-case RNG
bad: [gamedeveloper] Breaking psychological contract — players shouldn't predict *which* loot drops but should anticipate *when*
[gamedeveloper] Treating collation/drop tables as afterthought — feels exploitative, drives quitting
[gamedeveloper] Inverted cost curves (common costs more effort than uncommon) — destroys rarity perception
[gamedeveloper] Huge cost jumps between tiers — feels unfair, progression should be incremental
[gridsagegames] Over-randomization in fast-inventory games — players read stat blocks constantly, combat flow dies
[gridsagegames] Retrofitting randomization onto static-item codebase requires major refactoring — decide early
[hexworks] No stat caps = absurd stacked equipment — cap or soft-cap combined modifiers
[fandomwire/BL4] More part-farming = more grind — letting players chase specific parts creates parts-grind treadmill
[markrjohnson/URR] Don't sink dev time into one weapon family's visuals (he abandoned flails) — fix scope per class
[gamedev.net] Deep inheritance hierarchies for every unique effect become unmaintainable — prefer composition + data-driven
[cheveedodd] Naive string concatenation breaks on prepositions ('Staff Of Pride' vs 'Staff of Pride') — bake grammar in from day 1

gems.complaint: [steamcommunity BL2] Players report hundreds of runs with zero target drops and quit in frustration
gems.lesson: Soft pity system: track attempts against a target pool, progressively boost rarity weight — keep chase exciting but cap worst-case to ~2-3x average

gems.complaint: [gamedeveloper] 'Players would have quit right then' when probability produces long dry spells
gems.lesson: Every rarity tier needs guaranteed-within-N-drops ceiling. For Doge Gun Game: Epic within 20 kills, Legendary within 100

gems.complaint: [gridsagegames] 'Players frequently read and reread info' — randomization wrecks fast-paced shooters
gems.lesson: For PvP shooter, randomize FEW meaningful axes (1 element + 1 prefix + 1 suffix max), display compact 3-icon card — not a wall of stats

gems.complaint: [gamedeveloper] Poor loot design breaks psychological contract between player and RNG
gems.lesson: Show drop-rate odds upfront (tier % visible on UI), expose pity counter — 'game hates me' becomes '12/20 to guaranteed Epic'

gems.complaint: [gaming-fans 2026] EU Digital Fairness Act actively targeting 'gambling-like' features and dark-pattern loot boxes
gems.lesson: In 2026, design procedural loot as EARNED-through-play (kill X = guaranteed roll), not paid box — future-proof against regulation

gems.complaint: [fandomwire/BL4] Past players complained Legendaries had fixed rolls — finding 'bad' Legendary felt terrible
gems.lesson: Every rarity tier gets some roll variance so the same gun twice is meaningfully different — but let players reroll/swap one slot to prevent trash

good: [devforum:designing-ui-tips] Use Scale over Offset for responsive sizing across devices; anchor with UIAspectRatioConstraint + UISizeConstraint
[devforum:designing-ui-tips] Master AnchorPoint: (0.5,0.5) centers, (1,1) bottom-right; use scale values in Position for consistent placement
[devforum:designing-ui-tips] Test with Studio Device Emulator first; design mobile-first so UI scales up to larger screens
[devforum:designing-ui-tips] Respect 36px top bar + variable chat/leaderboard expansion; plan for both orientations
[devforum:designing-ui-tips] Use ScreenInsets to avoid Dynamic Island/notch obstruction on mobile safe areas
[devforum:designing-ui-tips] Reserve right side for default jump button and left for thumbstick — never overlap virtual controls
[devforum:mobile-buttons] Mobile buttons: 70x70 on small screens (<=500px), 120x120 on large screens
[devforum:mobile-buttons] Position custom mobile buttons relative to the default jump button, not fixed coords
[devforum:mobile-buttons] Detect input via LastInputTypeChanged, not TouchEnabled — avoids false mobile UI on touchscreen PCs
[devforum:mobile-friendly] Zero out Offset X/Y before applying Scale; AutoScale Lite plugin can convert existing UIs
[devforum:designing-ui-tips] Standard layouts: HUD at edges, modals for decisions, full-screen for pauses, toasts for transient alerts
[devforum:hud-feedback] Use UIGradients, patterns, and shaped ImageLabels (SliceScale, BackgroundTransparency=1) instead of flat rectangles
[devforum:hud-feedback] Color-code critical HUD values (loaded ammo) differently from secondary info to direct the eye
bad: [devforum:new-gen-ui-awful] Over-minimalism wasting screen real estate with centered tabs and empty margins
[devforum:new-gen-ui-awful] Hover-to-open panels trigger accidentally and destroy focus during play
[devforum:new-gen-ui-awful] Only extreme-contrast themes (pure bright or pure dark) with no middle ground — eye strain
[devforum:this-new-ui-ridiculous] Forced UI rollouts with no opt-out break muscle memory and kill productivity for veterans
[devforum:new-gen-ui-awful] Burying essential features so they take 20+ min to find instead of 2 clicks
[devforum:hud-feedback] Plain flat bars with no gradient, pattern, or color hierarchy read as unfinished
[devforum:mobile-buttons] Placing action buttons in left thumbstick zone conflicts with movement — misfires

gems.complaint: [devforum:new-gen-ui-awful] 'Super bright and super dark, no gray middle theme'
gems.lesson: Ship at least one neutral mid-contrast theme option; don't force either blinding white or pitch black

gems.complaint: [devforum:this-new-ui-ridiculous] Veterans resort to external loaders and old builds to keep working
gems.lesson: When redesigning UI for an established game, keep a 'classic mode' toggle for one release cycle so retention doesn't crater

gems.complaint: [devforum:new-gen-ui-awful] Hovering near a frame accidentally opens panels mid-action
gems.lesson: In a shooter HUD, never use hover-expanding panels during active gameplay — require explicit tap/click, auto-dismiss on movement

gems.complaint: [devforum:hud-feedback] Reviewer said ammo count looked plain though it's the most-watched number
gems.lesson: The single most-glanced HUD number (ammo, health, cooldown) gets its own color + size + gradient — never style like body text

gems.complaint: [devforum:mobile-buttons] Devs hardcode button coords, they collide with Roblox's default jump button on new phones
gems.lesson: Anchor custom mobile buttons relative to jump button's dynamic position, scale by screen min-axis (70 vs 120)

good: [pixel-art-convention] Restricted palette of 4-6 colors per 16x16 texture matches vanilla Minecraft readability
[pixel-art-convention] Hue shifts: shadows shift cooler/purple, highlights warmer/yellow — never just darken with black
[PIL-docs] Image.new('RGBA', (16,16), (0,0,0,0)) for transparent base; ImageDraw + putpixel for placement
[PIL-docs] Image.NEAREST when resampling/resizing preserves crisp pixel edges (default bicubic blurs)
[pixel-art-convention] Dither sparingly and only at palette transitions — heavy dithering reads as noise at 16x16
[pixel-art-convention] 1px darker outline on bottom/right edges of items matches vanilla's drop shadow convention
[minecraft-wiki] Block textures must tile seamlessly — test by placing 2x2 grid of same texture, check for seams
[PIL-palette] Build a named palette dict (PALETTE = {'shadow': (60,40,30,255), ...}) — keeps textures coherent across a mod
[fabric-docs] Textures in src/main/resources/assets/<modid>/textures/{block,item}/ — filenames must match registry ID exactly (lowercase, underscores)
[pixel-art-convention] Limit pure #000000 and #FFFFFF — use near-black and off-white for softer, more vanilla-consistent contrast
[PIL-tips] Save PNG with optimize=True and no color profile; strip metadata to keep jar small
[minecraft-wiki] Items use diagonal highlight convention (top-left bright, bottom-right dark); blocks use flat/axis-aligned shading
[PIL-tips] Generate a contact sheet PNG showing all mod textures on one grid to spot palette inconsistencies before packaging
bad: [common-mistake] Antialiased drawing (ImageDraw default) produces half-transparent edge pixels incompatible with pixel-art look
[common-mistake] Saving as JPEG or palette-indexed PNG without alpha — Minecraft expects RGBA PNG
[common-mistake] Mismatched texture resolutions (some 16x16, some 32x32) in the same mod — inconsistent next to vanilla
[common-mistake] Forgetting model JSON — a texture file alone does nothing without matching block/item model pointing at it
[common-mistake] Smooth gradients or soft shading that looks out of place against vanilla's hard-edged pixel shading
[common-mistake] Filenames with capital letters or spaces — resource loader is case-sensitive on Linux, silently fails
[common-mistake] Not clearing PIL image cache between generations in a loop — leaked pixels from previous texture

gems.complaint: [r/minecraftmods] 'My procedurally generated textures all look flat and boring next to vanilla'
gems.lesson: Vanilla has hand-placed noise on nearly every pixel — add a per-pixel ±10 RGB jitter pass clamped to palette after block-fill to emulate that texture density

gems.complaint: [pixel-art-convention] 'My mod's items look fine alone but clash with vanilla in the inventory'
gems.lesson: Sample vanilla item colors with eyedropper, constrain generated palette to hues within 15-20° of nearest vanilla analogue (wood, stone, iron)

gems.complaint: [PIL-forum] 'putpixel is extremely slow for batch texture generation'
gems.lesson: Build a numpy array (16,16,4) and convert with Image.fromarray(arr, 'RGBA') at the end — 100x+ faster for generating hundreds of textures

gems.complaint: [common-mistake] 'Weird fringe pixels around transparent areas'
gems.lesson: Premultiplied-alpha artifacts — always set transparent pixels to (0,0,0,0) not (255,255,255,0); mipmap blends RGB of fully-transparent pixels into edges

gems.complaint: [fabric-discord] 'Shadows look muddy when I just darken the base color'
gems.lesson: Convert to HSV, rotate hue -10 to -20° AND reduce value for shadows; rotate +10° AND increase value for highlights — 'hue shifting' makes pixel art pop

good: [devforum/25475] Guide players toward enemies via geometry, wider visible paths, lighting, and landmarks so they don't hunt
[devforum/25475] Block oppressive long sightlines with asset placement; prevent spawn-sniping angles
[devforum/25475] Ensure symmetric strategic options (snipe spots, CQB lanes, cover) even when aesthetics differ per side
[devforum/25475] Keep detail density uniform in reachable zones; only drop detail in far unreachable areas
[devforum/25475] Use contrasting team-side colors so players know which half they're in
[devforum/25475] Use heat maps of deaths/kills/movement to find camp spots and dead zones before shipping
[leveldesignbook] Err on too little cover in PvP — players improvise with grenades/abilities; over-cover stalls fights
[leveldesignbook] Distribute 3-4 chokepoints (one per lane); no single position covers all
[leveldesignbook] Vary sightline length across zones — mix deep and shallow so each weapon class has a home
[leveldesignbook] Rounded/beveled corners widen peek angles vs sharp 90s; use deliberately
[rblxgrind/Rivals 2026] Build maps rewarding loadout-swapping: CQB corridors + mid flanks + long sniper lanes in one map
[devforum/25475] Verticality creates vantage points, landmarks, occlusion, and style identity — don't build flat
[noping] Hand-build core architecture with Parts, only Toolbox complex props — keeps part count low
[YouTube 3-lane] 3-lane layout is the reliable skeleton — mid for power-weapon fights, sides for flanks
[leveldesignbook] Treat territory as fluid — design so control shifts, not one team locking the map down
bad: [devforum/25475] Forcing multiple turns away from spawn before first contact kills pacing
[devforum/25475] Hidden pathways with no visual emphasis — players never find flanks
[devforum/25475] Unblocked cross-map sniper lanes oppress every other weapon class
[leveldesignbook] Cookie-cutter waist-high 'cover boxes' feel artificial — read as Gmod blockout
[leveldesignbook] One dominant route/position making rest of map cosmetic
[noping] Grabbing unoptimized Toolbox models with thousands of tiny parts — crashes low-end devices
[devforum/25475] Symmetrical aesthetics without symmetrical gameplay options
[leveldesignbook] Mandatory trick-jump routes — turns risk into frustration, not skill expression

gems.complaint: [devforum/25475] 'Players end up surrounded by enemies without even realising it'
gems.lesson: Spawn orientation + team-side color cues must orient player in the first second — spawn facing the action, skybox/palette codes 'your half'

gems.complaint: [leveldesignbook] 'Prioritize fairness over balance'
gems.lesson: Playtest for felt fairness (did losing feel cheap?) not spreadsheet symmetry; asymmetric maps can feel fair if every lane has a counter-option

gems.complaint: [leveldesignbook] Optional flanking routes become mandatory when risk/reward is off
gems.lesson: If the flank is the only way to win, it's the main path in disguise — tune reward down or add a rival main route

gems.complaint: [devforum/424682] Devs oversize 20-player maps and matches feel empty
gems.lesson: Scale map to concurrent combatants, not player cap — fewer denser engagement nodes beat sprawling ghost-town geometry

gems.complaint: [leveldesignbook] 'Impossible to cover all chokepoints from a single point'
gems.lesson: Stress-test every elevated/central position — if one sightline watches 3+ chokes, break it with pillar, awning, rotated wall

good: [simplified.media] UpdateAsync for any field with concurrent access (gold, inventory, quest progress) — atomic read-modify-write, no lost updates
[simplified.media] Wrap every DataStore call in pcall with exponential backoff — 5 attempts base-2 for writes, 3 attempts base-1.5 for reads
[simplified.media] Always register game:BindToClose — up to 30s to flush final saves before shutdown
[simplified.media] Version your keys (PlayerData_v1, _v2) so schema migrations don't corrupt live profiles
[simplified.media] Save intervals by genre: simulators 60s, RPG 120s, MMO 180s; premium transactions save immediately
[simplified.media] Track observability metrics — success rate 99.9%+, p50/p99 latency, UpdateAsync conflict retries, BindToClose completion
[devforum/MadStudio] ProfileStore (successor to ProfileService) for session-locked player data — auto-save default 30s → 300s
[ProfileStore] MessagingService integration means another server taking the lock resolves in seconds, not minutes
[ProfileStore] Use Profile.LastSavedData for verification instead of legacy MetaTags pattern
[devforum] Reserve SetAsync only for initial data creation; everything else goes through UpdateAsync
[simplified.media] Respect ~60 + (10 × players) requests/minute budget and per-key 6-second write cap — batch, don't save-per-change
[simplified.media] Keep single values under 4 MB post-JSON, keys under 50 chars
[devforum] Validate data on save — exploiter-injected Instances or invalid types cause DataStore to throw and block saves
[ProfileStore] One profile per player — NOT for global leaderboards or shared state; use OrderedDataStore / MemoryStore for those
[devforum] Save on PlayerRemoving AND BindToClose; auto-save alone loses the last interval of progress
bad: [devforum] SetAsync every time a stat changes — blows rate limits and overwrites concurrent writes
[devforum] Overwriting real save with default template when GetAsync times out/errors — classic total-wipe bug; distinguish 'failed read' from 'new player'
[devforum] Skipping session locking in games with trading/teleports — causes item duplication
[simplified.media] Silently swallowing throttle errors without logging — throttles queue invisibly, cascade into lost saves
[devforum] Mutating schema in-place instead of bumping key version
[devforum] Trusting client-sent payloads and writing them straight to DataStore — Instance refs throw forever
[devforum] Using DataStore2 in 2026 — ProfileService/ProfileStore superseded it; backup model wastes calls and lacks true session locks
[devforum] Saving on every in-game action (coin pickup, XP tick) instead of batching to an interval

gems.complaint: [devforum] 'My player's entire save was wiped after a brief outage'
gems.lesson: #1 data-loss cause is NOT Roblox losing data — it's your code overwriting real data with default template after failed GetAsync. Treat pcall failure as 'don't save this session' NOT 'player is new'

gems.complaint: [devforum] 'Session locking but dupes still happen during teleports'
gems.lesson: ProfileService-era 30s auto-save meant lock handoff took 30+ seconds. ProfileStore + MessagingService resolves in ~1s — upgrade if your game has trading

gems.complaint: [simplified.media] 'UpdateAsync keeps retrying and I don't know why'
gems.lesson: UpdateAsync returning nil from the transform CANCELS the write. Useful to abort on validation failure but accidental nil = silent no-ops. Always return the table explicitly

gems.complaint: [devforum] 'BindToClose works in Studio but data still lost on live servers'
gems.lesson: BindToClose only gets 30s total across ALL yielding ops. 50 players saving sequentially at 6s/key throttle = 40+ players lost. Parallel-save with task.spawn + WaitAll pattern

gems.complaint: [ProfileStore] 'Tried ProfileStore for global leaderboard, it's slow/broken'
gems.lesson: ProfileStore is player-profile-only. For leaderboards use OrderedDataStore; for short-lived shared state (matchmaking, trades) use MemoryStore

good: [create.roblox.com] VoiceChatService.UseAudioApi = Enum.AudioApiRollout.Enabled = full control over voice routing, filtering, positioning via new Audio API graph
[devforum Audio API Beta] Audio API lets devs alter voices, implement team chat, walkie-talkies, custom spatial falloff
[devforum Speech-to-Text Feb 2026] Speech-to-Text API enables voice-controlled mechanics, accessibility captions, dynamic NPC conversations
[devforum GetChatGroupsAsync] TextChatService:GetChatGroupsAsync + teleport APIs cluster players by age-compatible chat tier before joining servers
[creator-docs voice-chat] Spatial Voice is proximity-based by default — volume attenuates with distance producing lifelike 3D presence
[devforum Zeph's push to talk] Community-proven push-to-talk module enhances spatial voice UX, reduces hot-mic noise
[ir.roblox.com 2026] Roblox is first major platform requiring global age-check for chat — design around verified/unverified tiers from day 1
[halosafe 2026] Real-time AI moderation flags critical-harm audio for human review; assume all voice is recorded and moderated
[devforum voice radio] Audio API AudioEmitter/AudioListener/AudioDeviceInput graph is the supported pattern for radios, megaphones, phones
[izoate 2026] Users 13+ can verify via ID as fallback to facial estimation — surface both paths in UX onboarding
[creator-docs] Restrict voice by group role/permission, cap MaxDistance to prevent cross-map eavesdropping
[roblox support] Voice eligibility must be checked per-player via VoiceChatService:IsVoiceEnabledForUserIdAsync before attaching audio objects
[devforum Help with VoiceChatService] Always guard voice setup in pcall — service yields and errors on unsupported regions/devices
bad: [devforum Voice Chat API 2022] Years with no developer API — legacy tutorials assume you can't mute/route per-player (now outdated)
[devforum complaints thread] 12-preset text chat cap and disallowed 'hello' make voice-adjacent text features unusable for trading/roleplay
[WIRED via hashtechwave] Facial age estimation reported as 'a complete mess' early 2026 — don't hard-gate flows on first-try success
[devforum region] Voice disabled in Russia and parts of Middle East regardless of age — plan graceful text fallback

gems.complaint: [devforum age-check] 'not being able to say hello is kinda dumb' — preset-only fallback chat blocks basic social glue
gems.lesson: Build gameplay coordination (pings, emotes, contextual radial menus) that doesn't rely on free-form text or voice at all for mixed verified/unverified lobbies

gems.complaint: [devforum] 'your guidelines do not matter, because you won't enforce them'
gems.lesson: Bake server-side voice permission checks, per-experience mute lists, and report hooks into your framework from v1 — don't assume platform moderation is sufficient

gems.complaint: [devforum VoiceChatService] Scripts error on platforms/regions where voice is disabled, breaking games silently
gems.lesson: Wrap every VoiceChatService call in pcall and feature-detect; degrade to spatial emotes/text when IsVoiceEnabledForUserIdAsync returns false

gems.complaint: [devforum Audio API Beta] Migrating legacy voice to AudioApi graph broke existing proximity setups
gems.lesson: Commit to UseAudioApi=Enabled early — mixing legacy channels with the new Audio graph causes duplicated audio and desync

gems.complaint: [halosafe 2026] Parents/kids confused about what's recorded; kids disable VC after verification failures
gems.lesson: Surface in-experience 'voice is moderated + recorded on flag' notice and a one-tap toggle; design parity — assume 30-50% play without voice

good: [CurseForge] L_Ender's Cataclysm treats bosses as set pieces — big arenas, multiple attack patterns, phases that force learning
[CurseForge] Bosses'Rise Infernal Dragon enters phase 2 at 1/3 HP, sheds skin to skeletal form, attacks faster, destroys arena floor
[CurseForge] Graveyard Corrupted Champion has heavy spell attacks + 'hunt phase' where darkness closes in while it stalks you
[Gamer Rant] Progressive Bosses: Wither charging dash triggered when player breaks LoS over 2s — prevents cheese
[CurseForge] Bosses'Rise Sandworm swallows player, forcing escape through internal stomach maze
[CurseForge] Stalwart Dungeons requires summoning bosses at altars with items — every fight intentional
[CurseForge] Blue Skies bosses scale HP/damage to number of players — enables co-op difficulty tuning
[CurseForge] Meet Your Fight uses crafted summoning items — each encounter feels earned through preparation
[CurseForge] Bosses'Rise adds combat roll for dodging telegraphed attacks — skill expression in vanilla combat
[Minecraft Guides] Bosses'Rise themed arena + thematically-consistent attack kit (Yeti=cold, Sandworm=desert, Underworld=Nether)
[CurseForge] Modular Bosses exposes HP, damage, attack frequency, rewards as config values — pack-tunable
[Twilight Forest] Progression loop — drops from one boss are the key/summon for the next — clear advancement
[CurseForge] Stalwart Dungeons Awful Ghast uses two-phase projectile-then-charge — switches distance engagement
[CurseForge] Performance stats in chat after fights (per-player damage/deaths) creates social stakes in multiplayer
bad: [Minecraft Feedback] Vanilla Wither/Ender Dragon called 'tedious' and 'lackluster' — little depth beyond DPS
[Minecraft Wiki] Warden 500HP + 16-45 damage but drops single sculk catalyst not Looting-boosted — reward/effort mismatch
[Quora] Pure bullet-sponge bosses with no mechanics — HP inflation without new patterns is filler
[Minecraft Feedback] Ender Dragon same fight every time — no variation or escalation
[DualShockers] Huge HP walls with no real threat — most-hated pattern across games
[Gamers Decide] Mod bosses relying on add-spawn as only mechanic — becomes tedious
[Reddit] One-shot attacks with no telegraph feel unfair, not hard

gems.complaint: [Minecraft Feedback] 'Boss fights need less boring more depth' — vanilla feels static
gems.lesson: Design at least 2 phases with clear visual/audio transition — players accept long fights if mechanics evolve

gems.complaint: [Quora] Bullet-sponge bosses frustrate because damage feels pointless until arbitrary HP threshold
gems.lesson: Tie HP thresholds to visible state changes (armor break, arena change, new attack) so every hit feels like progress

gems.complaint: [Gamer Rant] Players cheese Wither by hiding in 2x1 hole
gems.lesson: Add anti-cheese — line-of-sight timer triggering dash/teleport, or structure-breaking attack after N seconds of no engagement

gems.complaint: [Minecraft Wiki] Warden drops one item not boosted by Looting — fight not worth repeating
gems.lesson: Scale rewards to risk — unique guaranteed drops + loot table that rewards repeat kills keeps bosses part of economy

gems.complaint: [CurseForge] Many mod bosses drop you into empty flat arena with no hazards
gems.lesson: Arena itself is a mechanic — destructible floor, pillars for LOS, lava rings, shrinking darkness zones multiply difficulty without HP inflation

good: [EMI] Craftable mode lets players quickly craft any recipe they can afford from inventory — one-click execution removes friction
[EMI] Recipe tree visualization breaks complex crafts into steps showing base ingredients plus leftover materials
[EMI] Smart tag display shows 'Planks' instead of cycling through every variant — reduces cognitive noise
[EMI] Favorite both items AND recipes for rapid access
[EMI] Quick-craft keybinds route output straight into inventory or cursor, one or many at a time
[JEI] Scroll wheel navigates next/previous recipe pages
[JEI] [+] auto-fills crafting grid; Shift-Click stages multiple sets — tiered affordances casual vs bulk
[Polymorph] Recipe conflict chooser — button above output slot when ingredients match multiple recipes, lists all possible results

[CraftTweaker GUI] In-game recipe editor with plus-icon on category header + edit-icon per recipe
[Fabric Wiki] JSON recipe format under assets/<modid>/recipes/ named after output — predictable file lookup
[EMI] Drop-in JEI replacement keeps existing plugins working — migration path matters
bad: [Forge Forums] Crafting table GUI fails to open on right-click after version updates — event handler regressions
[Enigmatica6] Modded crafting stations can't substitute for vanilla crafting table for certain recipes — splits crafting surface

[Forge Forums] Output slot sometimes stays empty when grid matches valid recipe — silent failure, no error messaging

[MMCReviews] No keyboard shortcut for enabling tracking mode in EMI — core workflow buried behind menu clicks

gems.complaint: [MMCReviews] 'Lacks a shortcut for enabling tracking mode for a recipe'
gems.lesson: Every repeated action needs a keybind. If a user toggles it more than twice per session, a menu click is already too many

gems.complaint: [MCreator Forum] Custom crafting tables silently fail to produce output with no diagnostic
gems.lesson: Recipe-match failures must surface a reason (missing ingredient, wrong slot, unmet tag) — silent empty output is the worst UX

gems.complaint: [Enigmatica6] Mod crafting station incompatible with vanilla recipes
gems.lesson: Custom crafting UIs should inherit from vanilla recipe registry by default, override only where they add capability — don't fork the recipe space

gems.complaint: [MMCReviews] Recipe tree lags on complex crafts

gems.complaint: [Minecraft Forum] Shift-click exploit ejects items without consuming materials
gems.lesson: Bulk-craft shortcuts must be transactional — validate and consume ingredients in one atomic step, never split produce and consume paths

good: [TelepathicGrunt StructureTutorialMod] Multi-loader reference (NeoForge/Forge/Fabric branches) — minimum viable is 4 JSON + 1 NBT

gems.complaint: [Slynyrd] Beginners build 'straight ramps' — only brightness + saturation change, no hue shift — and ramps refuse to harmonize
gems.lesson: Every ramp needs hue rotation across its length (warm-to-cool or cool-to-warm). Tools that expose hue-shift slider per stop beat tools that only do HSL lightness steps

gems.complaint: [Derek Yu / Phonoforest] 'Biggest mistake is combining high saturation and brightness' — beginners max both and burn retinas
gems.lesson: Saturation peaks in MIDDLE of a ramp, not at the bright end. Bright colors desaturate toward white; dark colors desaturate toward a hue-shifted near-black — never flat gray

gems.complaint: [Aseprite Steam forum] Users hit palette-count confusion — indexed vs RGB mode, accidentally drifting off-palette mid-sprite
gems.lesson: Lock palette via indexed color mode before first stroke. Any pipeline that lets artists 'sample' off-palette colors mid-session guarantees palette drift

gems.complaint: [QWE AI] AI pixel-art generators output 'broken' — anti-aliased blur at wrong grid resolution with palettes that bleed
gems.lesson: AI is starting-point tool, not shipping tool. 2026 workflow: AI generates variations, artist picks one, palette quantized to fixed Lospec set, final polish in Aseprite

gems.complaint: [Slynyrd] Beginners attempt 64+ color master palettes up front and can't keep them coherent
gems.lesson: Build one 4-5 stop ramp first, validate on test sprite, derive sibling ramps by rotating hue on same brightness/saturation curve. Scale up only after template ramp works
